ci_guardrails.note: This manifest declares every seed fixture, raw capture, and expected category set used by the first cross-runtime parity tests. Hashes are intentionally deferred until generated by the local runner so runtime implementations do not hand-maintain digest evidence.

fixtures.description: Valid seed instance that should produce no raw errors and no canonical repair categories in either runtime.
negative_space: Does not prove semantic or symbolic correctness.
Does not prove production user data is valid.
Does not authorize network access during validation.

raw_captures.expected_raw_divergence: Ajv and python-jsonschema may expose different metadata even when both report zero errors.
Python may preserve JSONPath-style fields that Ajv does not expose.

fixtures.description: Invalid seed instance that forces required, type, and enum errors while keeping all content public-safe and abstract.
negative_space: Does not require raw validator messages to match.
Does not require raw validator error ordering to match.
Does not score meaning, diagnosis, emotion, identity, or private scenario truth.

raw_captures.expected_raw_divergence: Ajv exposes keyword, instancePath, schemaPath, and keyword-specific params; message text is debug-only.
python-jsonschema exposes validator, path/schema path, JSONPath-style location, and context-capable evidence; message text is debug-only.

purpose: Declare the first public-safe cross-runtime fixture pack so Python and Node parity tests can consume the same manifest, preserve divergent raw validator evidence, and compare only canonical repair category sets.
